name: admin_triage_errors_patch_string_status
_note: #37: Node binds raw body.status ('3' string) to the int4 column; Postgres coerces '3'->3 -> 200. Go forwards the raw text (::text::integer) not 400, and does NOT resolve (strict !== 4/5). status VALUE is a documented shadow-gate allowance: Node's TypeORM save() echoes the in-memory assigned value WITHOUT reloading, so a STRING input ('3') returns as JSON string "3"; Go reads the coerced int4 back from the DB -> number 3. Replicating Node's echo would need a struct-wide custom MarshalJSON (byte-parity risk on every other errreport route) to mirror a TypeORM artifact for an input the admin portal never sends (portal posts a typed number). Status code (200), resolution behavior, and all other fields still verified. last_seen_at=now() volatile.
